title: Instrument MCP interfaces with typed events
description: Add event recording to Fabric server MCP/service boundaries: plan-context records read-only planning events, rule-sections records selection/fetch events, get-rules legacy path if still present records rule access, and mutation/fix style services use typed events. Avoid using fab_append_intent as the recording path.
test: Service/tool tests assert typed Event Ledger records for plan_context, rule_sections, and existing audit-compatible rule selection flows.
acceptance_criteria: MCP calls write typed events with path, stable ids, diagnostics, reasons, and correlation/session ids where available; no task summary event is required; old audit-log writes are routed through Event Ledger compatibility.
hints: Do not remove deprecated tool registration here; focus on instrumentation for surviving interfaces || packages/server/src/tools/plan-context.ts;packages/server/src/services/rule-sections.ts;packages/server/src/services/audit-log.test.ts;packages/server/src/services/event-ledger.ts
prev_context:
[Explore mcp-instrumentation] MCP registration is split across packages/server/src/tools/* and calls services directly. Existing writes are service-local: getRules appends get_rules audit, getRuleSections appends rule_selection, appendIntent writes ledger then edit_intent audit; planContext is readOnlyHint true but has no event write, updateRegistry has none. Implement shared recordEvent for .fabric/events.jsonl and instrument plan_context_requested, rule_sections_selected, intent_recorded/edit_intent_checked, deprecated tool usage with correlation/session ids.
[Task T1] Added Event Ledger foundation.
[Task T2] readLedger/readAuditLog now merge Event Ledger projections with legacy files; audit-log append helpers dual-write compatible event records.

title: Implement doctor and sync-meta baseline event flow
description: Make rule text the source of truth in the implementation path: enhance doctor drift details, keep normal report read-only, and add explicit baseline acceptance/sync event recording from doctor fix or sync-meta when agents.meta.json hash baseline is updated.
test: Tests prove doctor report does not write events, explicit fix/sync writes rule_drift_detected/rule_baseline_accepted/baseline_synced events, and doctor error clears only after baseline sync or rollback.
acceptance_criteria: Doctor exposes enough drift detail for file/stable_id/expected_hash/actual_hash; sync-meta or doctor fix updates baseline and records typed events; normal doctor report remains non-mutating.
hints: Existing doctor --fix only migrates legacy ledger; keep read path clean || packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts;packages/cli/src/commands/sync-meta.ts;packages/shared/src/schemas/event-ledger.ts;packages/server/src/services/event-ledger.ts
execution_directives: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test -- doctor --runInBand; pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- sync-meta --runInBand
prev_context:
[Explore rule-baseline-doctor] sync-meta is the rule-text compiler and doctor recomputes hashes/revision from agents.meta.json. Existing doctor --fix only migrates legacy ledger. Event Ledger should record rule_drift_detected on explicit doctor audit/fix paths and rule_baseline_accepted when sync-meta updates meta; normal doctor reads stay non-mutating.
[Task T1] Added Event Ledger foundation.
[Task T2] .fabric/events.jsonl projections are available via readLedger/readAuditLog and event service.
